name: Alto Alentejo Portuguese
notes: Alentejo dialect of European Portuguese, covering the interior southern plains (Portalegre, Évora, Beja). Phonological profile: (1) open vowel qualities — stressed /e/ and /o/ realised as [ɛ] and [ɔ] more frequently than Lisbon standard; (2) vowel breaking [ɛ͡ɐ] in certain lexical environments (shared with some Greater Porto subdialects); (3) intermediate unstressed vowel reduction — less extreme than northern dialects but greater than standard Lisbon; (4) uvular rhotic [ʁ] consistent without coda devoicing; (5) /s/ and /z/ realised apico-alveolarly in older conservative speakers — a feature of possible seseo-adjacent transition zones near the Spanish border (Badajoz contact zone); (6) final /l/ vocalised to [w] as in standard EP; (7) lexical prosody: strong stress on penultimate syllable, broader vowel duration. The Alto Alentejo (Portalegre/Crato) forms a transitional zone between northern and southern EP dialect blocks as defined by Cintra (1971). Minho-type vowel breaking [ɛ͡ɐ] in the north of the region is diagnostically significant.

ancestors.notes: Standard European Portuguese; Alentejo diverges mainly in open vowel quality and transitional seseo-adjacent features near the Spanish border

sources.author: Boléo, M.P.
sources.title: Estudos de linguística portuguesa e românica
